stories.title: Two SonicWall SMA 1000 Zero-Days Exploited, One Could Enable Admin Commands
stories.abstract: The Inc ransomware group is actively exploiting a chain of critical vulnerabilities in SonicWall SMA 1000 series appliances. These flaws allow unauthenticated remote attackers to perform server-side request forgery and execute arbitrary commands, ultimately granting them root-level access to the targeted devices.
stories.why: These vulnerabilities provide attackers with full control over edge infrastructure, facilitating network infiltration and ransomware deployment.
stories.action: Apply the latest platform hotfixes provided by SonicWall for SMA 1000 series appliances immediately.

stories.title: CISA Adds Exploited SharePoint RCE Zero-Day CVE-2026-58644 to KEV
stories.abstract: CISA has added several vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, most notably a critical deserialization flaw in Microsoft SharePoint (CVE-2026-58644) and command injection issues affecting Fortinet FortiSandbox, all of which are currently being leveraged in active cyberattacks.
stories.why: These vulnerabilities are actively exploited in the wild and pose a high risk of remote code execution or unauthorized system access.
stories.action: Apply the latest security updates for Microsoft SharePoint and Fortinet FortiSandbox immediately.

stories.title: Fortinet security advisory (AV26-351) – Update 2
stories.abstract: CISA has added multiple Fortinet vulnerabilities, including CVE-2026-25089 and CVE-2026-39808, to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. These flaws affect various FortiSandbox, FortiAnalyzer, and FortiManager versions, enabling OS command injection attacks.
stories.why: These vulnerabilities are actively exploited in the wild, posing a significant risk of unauthorized system access and remote code execution.
stories.action: Update FortiSandbox, FortiAnalyzer, and FortiManager to the latest patched versions immediately.

stories.title: Researcher Drops New Windows Zero-Day PoC Hours After Microsoft Patch Tuesday
stories.abstract: A researcher has published a proof-of-concept exploit named LegacyHive that targets the Windows User Profile Service. The vulnerability allows local attackers to escalate privileges by manipulating how the system handles hive loading, affecting fully patched Windows environments.
stories.why: Privilege escalation flaws in core Windows services allow attackers to bypass security boundaries and gain administrative control over compromised endpoints.

stories.title: CISA urges immediate action on actively exploited Fortinet flaws
stories.abstract: CISA has issued a directive requiring federal agencies to remediate two critical vulnerabilities affecting the Fortinet FortiSandbox platform. These security flaws are currently being leveraged in active exploitation campaigns, necessitating immediate attention to prevent unauthorized access or system compromise.
stories.why: Active exploitation of critical infrastructure components poses a significant risk of network intrusion and data exfiltration for organizations utilizing Fortinet solutions.

stories.title: Dairy company Fairlife suspends production in US after cyber incident
stories.abstract: Coca-Cola has halted operations at its Fairlife dairy facilities across the United States following a ransomware attack. The company is currently investigating the scope of the breach and the extent of the operational disruption.
stories.why: Large-scale food production outages often indicate significant infrastructure compromise, potentially impacting supply chains and sensitive corporate data.

stories.title: Three Steps to the Terminal: A Siemens ROX II Zero-Day Trilogy
stories.abstract: Researchers identified a chain of three zero-day vulnerabilities affecting Siemens ROX II industrial switches. By exploiting these flaws, an attacker can escalate privileges and establish persistent root-level access to the device's operating system.
stories.why: Compromised industrial switches provide attackers with a foothold to manipulate critical infrastructure networks and bypass traditional security perimeters.
stories.action: Contact Siemens support for firmware updates or mitigation guidance regarding ROX II devices.

stories.title: Your Period Tracker Is (Probably) Spying on You
stories.abstract: Recent reports highlight significant privacy concerns regarding period tracking applications, which frequently harvest sensitive user health data for third-party advertising and analytics purposes, often without transparent consent or robust security protections for highly personal information.
stories.why: Health data is highly sensitive; unauthorized exposure or monetization of this information creates significant privacy risks and potential for targeted exploitation.

stories.title: Microsoft Edge security advisory (AV26-714)
stories.abstract: Microsoft and Google have released security updates for Edge and Chrome to address multiple vulnerabilities. Additionally, Notepad++ has issued a patch for versions prior to 9.7 to resolve identified security flaws.
stories.why: These updates address browser-based vulnerabilities that could potentially lead to remote code execution or system compromise if left unpatched.
stories.action: Update Edge to 150.0.4078.80, Chrome to 150.0.7871.128, and Notepad++ to 9.7.

stories.title: ABB Ability Edgenius
stories.abstract: ABB has released patches for its Ability Edgenius platform to address a critical Linux kernel vulnerability known as Copy Fail. This flaw allows locally authenticated users or compromised containers to escalate privileges to root, granting full control over the affected gateway and server hardware.
stories.why: Privilege escalation vulnerabilities in industrial control gateways provide attackers with a direct path to manipulate critical infrastructure and OT environments.
stories.action: Update ABB Ability Edgenius software to version 3.2.4.1 or later.

stories.title: 23andMe to pay $18 million in new genetics data breach settlement
stories.abstract: 23andMe will pay $18 million to settle claims from multiple U.S. states regarding a 2023 data breach. The agreement mandates that the company implement stricter security protocols and improved data protection measures to safeguard sensitive genetic information moving forward.
stories.why: It highlights the increasing legal and regulatory consequences for companies failing to secure highly sensitive biometric and genetic data.

stories.title: Prompt Injection Attacks Are Thwarting AI Hacking Agents
stories.abstract: Researchers have identified a defensive technique called context bombing that effectively neutralizes autonomous AI hacking agents. By injecting specific prompts, defenders can force these malicious tools to terminate their processes prematurely, preventing them from executing harmful commands or completing unauthorized tasks.
stories.why: This demonstrates a viable method for disrupting automated AI-driven cyberattacks, highlighting the vulnerability of agentic workflows to adversarial prompt manipulation.

stories.title: OpenSSL HollowByte Flaw Could Freeze Server Memory with 11-Byte TLS Requests
stories.abstract: Researchers identified a denial-of-service vulnerability in OpenSSL, dubbed HollowByte, where an 11-byte TLS request causes servers to permanently allocate significant memory. This resource exhaustion persists until the process is restarted, potentially leading to system instability or crashes.
stories.why: Unauthenticated attackers can easily trigger this memory leak, leading to service disruption on affected infrastructure without requiring complex exploit chains.
stories.action: Update OpenSSL to the latest version to incorporate the silent fix released in June.

stories.title: In Other News: Iran Tracks US Military Phones, CrashStealer macOS Malware, CVD Blueprint
stories.abstract: Recent security incidents include the exploitation of OpenClaw AI agents through WhatsApp, a ransomware attack targeting naval defense contractor TKMS, and a confirmed data breach at the retail chain Lidl.
stories.why: These incidents highlight ongoing risks to supply chain integrity, AI-integrated communication platforms, and the protection of sensitive corporate and consumer data.

stories.title: Broadcom VMware security advisory (AV26-712)
stories.abstract: Broadcom released security updates for VMware Avi Load Balancer to resolve seven distinct vulnerabilities. These flaws, tracked as CVE-2026-47865 through CVE-2026-47871, affect multiple versions of the product and require immediate attention from administrators to maintain system integrity.
stories.why: Load balancers are critical infrastructure components; unpatched vulnerabilities here can lead to unauthorized access or full system compromise.
stories.action: Review VMSA-2026-0005 and apply the latest patches for VMware Avi Load Balancer.

stories.title: FreePBX security advisory (AV26–711)
stories.abstract: FreePBX has released security updates addressing multiple vulnerabilities across several modules, including UCP, missedcall, tts, music, and the core framework for versions 16 and 17. Administrators should review the specific module versions to ensure their deployments are patched against these identified flaws.
stories.why: Unpatched VoIP infrastructure is a frequent target for unauthorized access, toll fraud, and eavesdropping.
stories.action: Update FreePBX modules to the latest versions specified in the advisory.

stories.title: Abbott probes two cyber incidents amid extortion claims
stories.abstract: Abbott Laboratories is currently investigating two distinct security breaches. The incidents involve unauthorized access to legacy systems within its cancer diagnostics division and a separate compromise of the LabCentral portal, where attackers reportedly exfiltrated sensitive corporate data.
stories.why: Healthcare organizations remain high-value targets for data extortion, and legacy system vulnerabilities continue to provide persistent entry points for threat actors.

stories.title: Ernst & Young discloses data breach after support system hack
stories.abstract: Ernst & Young confirmed a security incident involving a third-party IT support ticketing platform. Unauthorized actors accessed the system, potentially exposing sensitive information belonging to the firm's clients and personnel.
stories.why: Supply chain compromises via third-party vendors remain a primary vector for unauthorized data exfiltration and lateral movement into enterprise environments.

stories.title: Two Scattered Spider Hackers Get 5.5 Years Each for £29 Million TfL Hack
stories.abstract: Two members of the Scattered Spider threat group received five-and-a-half-year prison sentences for their roles in the 2024 cyberattack against Transport for London. The breach crippled over 140 internal systems and forced a massive, manual password reset for the organization's entire workforce.
stories.why: This case highlights the operational impact of Scattered Spider's social engineering and extortion tactics on critical infrastructure and large-scale enterprise environments.

stories.title: Armenia Detains Russian Tourist on U.S. Warrant for REvil Hacker, Lawyers Say Wrong Man
stories.abstract: Armenian authorities detained a Russian tourist at Yerevan airport following a U.S. extradition request targeting a suspected REvil ransomware operator. The detainee's legal representatives and family claim officials have apprehended the wrong individual, citing a case of mistaken identity.
stories.why: This highlights the complexities of international law enforcement cooperation and the potential for significant errors in cross-border cybercrime suspect identification.

stories.title: Rockwell Automation CompactLogix, ControlLogix, Compact GuardLogix and GuardLogix
stories.abstract: Rockwell Automation has disclosed multiple vulnerabilities across several Logix controller series, Flex 5000 adapters, and 1715-AENTR modules. These flaws range from denial-of-service conditions to critical authentication bypasses that allow unauthorized file manipulation, memory modification, and control over I/O states.
stories.why: These vulnerabilities impact critical infrastructure, potentially allowing attackers to disrupt industrial processes or gain unauthorized control over sensitive automation hardware.
stories.action: Review Rockwell Automation security advisories and apply available firmware updates to affected controllers and adapters.

stories.title: Rockwell Automation FactoryTalk DataMosaix
stories.abstract: Rockwell Automation has disclosed multiple vulnerabilities affecting FactoryTalk DataMosaix and Arena Simulation software. These flaws include stored cross-site scripting in DataMosaix and several out-of-bounds write issues in Arena that could lead to arbitrary code execution.
stories.why: These vulnerabilities impact critical manufacturing infrastructure, potentially allowing attackers to compromise industrial systems through memory corruption or malicious script injection.
stories.action: Update FactoryTalk DataMosaix and Arena Simulation to the latest patched versions provided by Rockwell Automation.

stories.title: Rockwell Automation 1756-EN2, 1756-EN3, and 1756-ENBT
stories.abstract: Rockwell Automation communication modules, specifically the 1756-EN2, 1756-EN3, and 1756-ENBT series, are vulnerable to a denial-of-service attack. Improper validation of CIP Implicit Connection packets allows remote network attackers to disrupt module operations.
stories.why: This flaw impacts critical infrastructure, potentially halting industrial control processes by crashing network communication modules.
stories.action: Update affected 1756-EN2, 1756-EN3, and 1756-ENBT modules to the latest patched firmware versions.

stories.title: AutomationDirect Productivity Suite
stories.abstract: Multiple vulnerabilities in AutomationDirect Productivity Suite versions 4.6.2.2 and earlier could allow local or physical attackers to trigger memory corruption, data exposure, application crashes, or denial-of-service conditions through out-of-bounds read/write errors and divide-by-zero flaws.
stories.why: These flaws impact industrial control software, potentially disrupting critical manufacturing operations if exploited by an attacker with local system access.
stories.action: Update Productivity Suite to a version newer than 4.6.2.2.

stories.title: GoldenEyeDog Subgroup Linked to DigiCert Breach and Code-Signing Certificate Theft
stories.abstract: Researchers identified a subgroup of the Chinese cybercrime collective GoldenEyeDog, known as CylindricalCanine, as the entity responsible for the April 2026 breach at DigiCert. The intrusion resulted in the theft of sensitive code-signing certificates.
stories.why: Compromised code-signing infrastructure allows attackers to distribute malicious software that appears legitimate and trusted by operating systems and security tools.

stories.title: The Future of Age Verification: Your Face Never Leaves Your Device
stories.abstract: Incode is promoting an on-device age estimation technology designed to verify user age locally. By processing facial data directly on the hardware rather than transmitting it to external servers, the system aims to satisfy regulatory compliance while minimizing biometric data exposure.
stories.why: Centralized biometric databases are high-value targets; shifting verification to the edge reduces the risk of massive data breaches and privacy litigation.

stories.title: SALTO ProAccess Space
stories.abstract: SALTO ProAccess Space versions prior to 6.13 contain an authorization bypass vulnerability. Authenticated operators can exploit this flaw to escalate privileges and access restricted partitions within the same system, provided the partitioning feature is enabled.
stories.why: This flaw allows unauthorized lateral movement and access to sensitive areas within physical security management systems, potentially impacting critical infrastructure.
stories.action: Update SALTO ProAccess Space to version 6.13 or later.

stories.title: Gold Eagle Clearinghouse Targets Security Gap, but How Is Unclear
stories.abstract: The White House has introduced the Gold Eagle initiative, a framework designed to streamline vulnerability identification and remediation processes in the context of artificial intelligence. However, the specific operational mechanics and implementation strategies for this program remain largely undefined.
stories.why: It signals a shift in federal vulnerability management policy, potentially impacting how organizations prioritize and report security flaws in AI-integrated systems.

stories.title: Siemens SICAM 8
stories.abstract: Siemens has identified four vulnerabilities in its SICAM 8 product line, including the A8000, EGS, and S8000 series. These flaws, which involve insecure resource initialization and unverified password changes, could allow remote attackers to trigger a denial-of-service condition on critical infrastructure equipment.
stories.why: These vulnerabilities impact industrial control systems, potentially disrupting critical operations if exploited by unauthorized actors.
stories.action: Update SICAM 8 firmware to version 26.20 or later.

stories.title: NASA Core Flight System (cFS) Health & Safety (HS) Application
Windows 0-day drops the same day Microsoft releases record number of patches
stories.abstract: Microsoft issued a massive batch of security updates, but researchers simultaneously identified a new zero-day vulnerability dubbed HiveLegacy. This flaw functions as a potent primitive, potentially enabling attackers to perform unauthorized operations beyond its initial observed capabilities.
stories.why: Zero-day exploits targeting core Windows primitives pose significant risks for privilege escalation and persistent system compromise.

stories.title: Government Agencies Falling Victim to Ransomware Daily, Warns Study
stories.abstract: A new report highlights that public sector entities are facing near-daily ransomware attacks. Threat actors are specifically targeting government infrastructure, banking on the critical nature of public services to force rapid ransom payments and minimize operational downtime.
stories.why: Government agencies remain high-value targets for extortionists due to their reliance on legacy systems and the urgent need to restore public-facing services.
